ci_support_linux_64_python3.10.____cpyth:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version

about.description:
The silx project aims at providing a collection of Python packages to
support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis
applications at synchrotron radiation facilities. It aims at providing
reading/writing different file formats, data reduction routines and a set
of Qt widgets to browse and visualize data.
about.summary: Silx tool-kit: collection of Python packages to support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis applications at synchrotron radiation facilities

name: silx-base

about.description:
The silx project aims at providing a collection of Python packages to
support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis
applications at synchrotron radiation facilities. It aims at providing
reading/writing different file formats, data reduction routines and a set
of Qt widgets to browse and visualize data.
about.summary: Silx tool-kit: collection of Python packages to support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis applications at synchrotron radiation facilities

about.description:
The silx project aims at providing a collection of Python packages to
support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis
applications at synchrotron radiation facilities. It aims at providing
reading/writing different file formats, data reduction routines and a set

about.summary: Silx tool-kit: collection of Python packages to support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis applications at synchrotron radiation facilities

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: 3.0.0
  build_number: '0'

recipe:
  name: silx-split
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
- url: https://pypi.org/packages/source/s/silx/silx-${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: c40c084a134e55103be9f3d51ebc7ee8834cf0c68a6e63fcb1fb5de5b8725e91

build:
  number: ${{ build_number }}
  skip: match(python, "<3.10")

outputs:
  - package:
      name: silx-base
    build:
      script:
        # Cross-compilation stuff vendored from numpy-feedstock
        # necessary for cross-compilation to point to the right env
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - export PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$PREFIX/lib/pkgconfig
            - echo "python = '${PREFIX}/bin/python'" >> ${CONDA_PREFIX}/meson_cross_file.txt
        - if: unix
          then:
            - MESON_ARGS_REDUCED="$(echo $MESON_ARGS | sed 's/--buildtype release //g')"
            - ${{ PYTHON }} -m pip install -vv --no-deps --ignore-installed . -Csetup-args=${MESON_ARGS_REDUCED// / -Csetup-args=}
          else: ${{ PYTHON }} -m pip install -vv --no-deps --ignore-installed .
      python:
        entry_points:
        - silx = silx.__main__:main
    requirements:
      build:
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - python
            - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
            - cython
            - meson-python
            - ninja
        - ${{ compiler('c') }}
        - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - if: osx
          then: llvm-openmp
        - if: linux
          then: libgomp
      host:
        - python
        - pip
        - cython
        - meson-python
        - if: osx
          then: llvm-openmp
        - if: linux
          then: libgomp
      run:
        - python
        - numpy
        - packaging
        - h5py >=3
        - fabio
        - pydantic >=2
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
          - silx
          - silx.image.bilinear
          - silx.image.marchingsquares
          - silx.image.shapes
          - silx.io.specfile
          - silx.math.colormap
          - silx.math.combo
          - silx.math.fit
          - silx.math.histogram
          - silx.math.interpolate
          - silx.math.marchingcubes
          - silx.math.medianfilter
          pip_check: true

  - package:
      name: silx
    build:
      noarch: generic
    requirements:
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('silx-base', exact=True) }}
        # opencl optional dependencies
        - pyopencl
        - mako
        # full_no_qt
        - qtconsole
        - matplotlib-base >=3.6
        - pyopengl
        - python-dateutil
        - scipy
        - pooch
        - pillow
        - pint
        - hdf5plugin
        - qtawesome
        # full
        # pyside6 and pyqt6 unavailable for ppc64le
        - if: linux and ppc64le
          then: pyqt
          else: pyside6 >= 6.5
    tests:
      - python:
          pip_check: true
      - script:
        - silx --help

about:
  license: MIT
  license_file: copyright
  homepage: https://www.silx.org/
  repository: https://github.com/silx-kit/silx
  documentation: http://www.silx.org/doc/silx/latest/
  summary: 'Silx tool-kit: collection of Python packages to support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis applications at synchrotron radiation facilities'
  description: |
    The silx project aims at providing a collection of Python packages to
    support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis
    applications at synchrotron radiation facilities. It aims at providing
    reading/writing different file formats, data reduction routines and a set
    of Qt widgets to browse and visualize data.

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - CJ-Wright
    - tschoonj
    - vallsv
    - t20100

about.description:
The silx project aims at providing a collection of Python packages to
support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis
applications at synchrotron radiation facilities. It aims at providing
reading/writing different file formats, data reduction routines and a set
of Qt widgets to browse and visualize data.
about.summary: Silx tool-kit: collection of Python packages to support the development of data assessment, reduction and analysis applications at synchrotron radiation facilities
